mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0%, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and industrial production signal a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average indicates robust supply-side capacity expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) reflect increased household liquidity buffers, which historically precede stronger consumer discretionary spending and equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return median is non-positive across all rolling 10-year windows, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand that validates economic resilience, prompting institutional rebalancing into equities and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward positive values when the payroll threshold is breached, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding the purchasing power of fixed-income assets and forcing portfolio reallocation into equities, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not systematically outperform when the inflation-funds rate spread widens, or the interaction effect vanishes after adjusting for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized rate indicates an economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return median remains positive when GDP growth falls below 1.5%, or the predictive power of the GDP growth threshold disappears in a 5-year rolling window.
